mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single quarter, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real retail sales indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, elevated inflation expectations erode real yields and compress equity risk premiums, causing the S&P 500 to underperform over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during identified tightening episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expansion directly increases corporate revenue streams, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate positively with subsequent 20-day equity returns, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in the industrial production index below 105 signals supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that suppress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO drops below the 105 threshold, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and stagnant real retail sales indicates a wealth effect decoupling that precedes consumer spending pullbacks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The employment-consumption divergence fails to predict negative 60-day equity returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic remains below 1.96 in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market downturn, declining housing starts reduce construction-related corporate earnings and household wealth, causing the S&P 500 to underperform over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral during quarters with housing starts declines exceeding 10%, or the predictive Rank IC stays below 0.02 over a 4-year rolling window.
